true_header: Any information which is or in future comes into the public domain (unless as a result of the breach of this agreement);
Any information which is already known to the recipient and which was not subject to any obligation of confidence before it was disclosed to the recipient by the discloser.
The recipient will, on request from the discloser,
Neither this agreement nor the supply of any information grants the recipient any licence,
This agreement is governed by, and is to be construed in accordance with,
The fact that discussions and negotiations are taking place concerning the purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations;
All  confidential  or  proprietary  information  relating  to:
Confidential information does not include information which:
Which  is  or  subsequently  becomes  public  knowledge  or  publicly  available  through  no  fault  of  the receiving party;
Which  is  known  by  the  receiving  party  prior  to  the  time  of  receipt,
Which  is  lawfully  obtained  by  the  receiving  party  from  a  third  party  and  is  not  subject  to  a  similar restriction on disclosure;
Which is independently developed by receiving party; or
The parties agree in writing is not confidential.
Keep the confidential information secret and confidential,
Not use or exploit the confidential information in any way except for the purpose;
Not disclose or make available any confidential information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this agreement;
To establish and maintain adequate security measures to safeguard the confidential information from unauthorized access or use.
To its and its affiliate’s representatives,
As may be required by an order of any court of competent jurisdiction or governmental body in which case the receiving party shall,
Return or destruction of confidential information
Upon  the  disclosing  party’s  written  request,
Clause 5.1 of this agreement shall not apply to:
Confidential  information  held  electronically  in  archive  or  back-up  systems  which  are  not  otherwise reasonably retrievable by the representatives of the receiving party or its affiliates;
Copies  of  confidential  information  which  must  be  retained  by  the  receiving  party  pursuant  to applicable law.
Each party reserves all rights in its confidential information.
No obligation to disclose or continue discussions
Term and survival of confidentiality obligations
To keep the disclosing party’s confidential information, confidential;
Not to use the disclosing party’s confidential information except in connection with the purpose; and
Not to disclose the disclosing party’s confidential information to  anyone else.
This agreement will not apply to information which:
Has been received from  someone else who owes no duty of confidence in relation to it;
Was already known by the receiving party.
Nothing in this agreement will prevent the receiving party from disclosing confidential information:
If the disclosing party has given its prior written consent; or
If the disclosure is required by law, regulation or professional requirements;
The receiving party will ensure that anyone who receives confidential information under clause 2.
Following a written request from the disclosing party, the receiving party will,
"affiliate" means with respect to any person,
Agreement means this non-disclosure agreement including all appendices (if any).
Client  means any customers or business partners of the party;
Effective date means the date when the last of the parties signed the agreement.
Project has the meaning ascribed to it in clause 4.1.
Use confidential information only for the project;
Was, at the time of its disclosure,
Breach by recipient of its obligations contained in this agreement (or by any person to whom disclosure of information is made as permitted under this agreement);
Breach by a third party of any other obligation or duty of confidentiality or non- disclosure relating to that information that recipient is or ought to be aware of;
Is required to be disclosed by law,
Inform discloser of the circumstances of the disclosure and the information that will be disclosed as soon as reasonably practicable;
Permit discloser in its absolute discretion to seek to obtain an injunction or take other appropriate action to protect the confidential information;
Take all such steps as may be reasonable and practicable in the circumstances to agree the form,
Consult with discloser as to possible steps to prevent or limit such disclosure and take those steps to the extent reasonably practicable in the circumstances,
Request assurances as to confidentiality from anybody to which the confidential information is to be disclosed.
With respect to the confidential information disclosed,
If recipient discloses or distributes confidential information to any of its representatives,
